lines.line: # Introduction and Mansfield Tilly

> The interview begins with a discussion of Dolores' cousin, Mansfield Tilly, who was the first black electrician in Bloomfield and possibly the state, and his influence in joining unions and building a home on Cottage Grove Road.
** introduction, Mansfield Tilly, electrician, cousin, Bloomfield, union **
keywords: ** introduction, Mansfield Tilly, electrician, cousin, Bloomfield, union **

metadata.title: Introduction and Mansfield Tilly
metadata.synopsis: The interview begins with a discussion of Dolores' cousin, Mansfield Tilly, who was the first black electrician in Bloomfield and possibly the state, and his influence in joining unions and building a home on Cottage Grove Road.
metadata.keywords: introduction, Mansfield Tilly, electrician, cousin, Bloomfield, union

lines.line: # Dolores’ Journey to Bloomfield

> Dolores explains her move to Bloomfield, influenced by her childhood visits and her cousin's residence. She contrasts her housing options and reflects on the rural charm of the area compared to neighboring West Hartford.
** Bloomfield, move, childhood, cousin, West Hartford **
keywords: ** Bloomfield, move, childhood, cousin, West Hartford **

metadata.title: Dolores’ Journey to Bloomfield
metadata.synopsis: Dolores explains her move to Bloomfield, influenced by her childhood visits and her cousin's residence. She contrasts her housing options and reflects on the rural charm of the area compared to neighboring West Hartford.
metadata.keywords: Bloomfield, move, childhood, cousin, West Hartford

lines.line: # Raising a Family in Bloomfield
> Dolores talks about raising her two sons in Bloomfield, their schooling, and her husband's architectural business. She also discusses the community, safety, and neighborly connections in the area.
** family, sons, schools, husband, architectural business, community, safety **
keywords: ** family, sons, schools, husband, architectural business, community, safety **

metadata.title: Raising a Family in Bloomfield
metadata.synopsis: Dolores talks about raising her two sons in Bloomfield, their schooling, and her husband's architectural business. She also discusses the community, safety, and neighborly connections in the area.
metadata.keywords: family, sons, schools, husband, architectural business, community, safety

lines.line: # The Allure of Bloomfield

> Dolores elaborates on the charm of Bloomfield, with its friendly people, youth and senior programs, and its recognition as a former All-American City, emphasizing the comfortable and supportive community.
** Bloomfield, community, All-American City, programs **

metadata.title: The Allure of Bloomfield
metadata.synopsis: Dolores elaborates on the charm of Bloomfield, with its friendly people, youth and senior programs, and its recognition as a former All-American City, emphasizing the comfortable and supportive community.

lines.line: # Living in Bloomfield in the 70s and 80s
> Recalling the 1970s and 1980s, Dolores describes Bloomfield as an active and welcoming community with abundant child-friendly activities and positive race relations in a multicultural environment.
** Bloomfield, 1970s, 1980s, activities, multicultural, race relations **

metadata.title: Living in Bloomfield in the 70s and 80s
metadata.synopsis: Recalling the 1970s and 1980s, Dolores describes Bloomfield as an active and welcoming community with abundant child-friendly activities and positive race relations in a multicultural environment.

lines.line: # Changes in Bloomfield's Demographics
> Dolores discusses the demographic shifts in Bloomfield, noting the increase in diversity, with more people of color and islanders, and its impact on the multicultural fabric and education system.
** Bloomfield, demographics, diversity, education **

metadata.title: Changes in Bloomfield's Demographics
metadata.synopsis: Dolores discusses the demographic shifts in Bloomfield, noting the increase in diversity, with more people of color and islanders, and its impact on the multicultural fabric and education system.

lines.line: # A Passion for Painting

> Dolores shares her journey into painting, inspired by a promise made in college, and her subsequent studies with various artists. She details her art style influenced by Impressionism and her teaching experiences.
** painting, art journey, Impressionism, teaching, artists **
keywords: ** painting, art journey, Impressionism, teaching, artists **

story.title: Dolores Howard Oral History Part 2 03172023.WAV
story.description: The interview discusses Dolores' life in Bloomfield, highlighting her cousin Mansfield Tilly's significance as a pioneering Black electrician. Dolores reflects on her 52-year residency, emphasizing Bloomfield's appeal due to its beauty, community, and proximity to family. She notes demographic changes over time and their impact on local education, expressing a desire for improved multicultural engagement. Dolores shares her journey into painting, influenced by her studies and collaborations with various artists, leading to her teaching painting classes. Her artistic style is inspired by Impressionists, and she values creativity through evolving artistic expressions.
